extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I counted only the “Against our Company,” “Against our Promoter,” and “Against our Directors” rows, with criminal counts of Nil, statutory/regulatory counts of Nil, and tax counts of 6, 1, and 4 respectively; the tax field is restricted to the company and subsidiaries, so only the company’s 6 tax proceedings are counted, while promoter/director tax matters are excluded. For the aggregate amount against the company/group, I used the company row’s quantified amount of ₹132.41 million and excluded promoter and director amounts; this converts to ₹13.241 crore (132.41 ÷ 10), with no subsidiary row disclosed. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated Total of ₹205.92 million as at March 31, 2024, converted to ₹20.592 crore (205.92 ÷ 10), rather than a re-sum of line items. The company-wide customer concentration table reports Fiscal 2024 percentages as a percentage of total revenue from operations: top 5 customers account for 28.82% and top 10 customers account for 38.11%. The source currency unit for the litigation and contingent-liability tables is ₹ million; all crore fields are converted using 1 crore = ₹10 million.
